TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua ti Tjukutjuku a pinatsayan nua kama nua kina, aya a milimilingan.
S.PHON: izua ti cukucuku a pinaʦajan nua kama nua kina, aja a miɭimiɭiŋan.
S.FORM: izua ti Tjukutjuku a pinacayan nua kama nua kina, aya a milimilingan.
S.PHON: izua ti ʦukuʦuku a pinaʦajan nua kama nua kina, aja a miɣimiɣiŋan.
S.TRANSL: There was a person called Tjukutjuku whose father and mother had died, according to the old story.

S.FORM: saka izua ti sa Tjamuakakay, saka galemgemi azua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: saka izua ti sa camuakakaj, saka gaɭəmgəmi azua ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: saka izua ti sa Tjamuakakay, saka galemgemi azua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: saka izua ti sa ʦamuakakaj, saka gaɣəmgəmi azua ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: There was also a person called Tjamuakakay (or Muakakay, her aunt, OA), who disliked Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: saka zua vavayan a kakeḍian puɫatan tua nanemanemanga tua qinaɫan tua kadjunangan.
S.PHON: saka zua vavajan a kakəɖian puʎatan tua nanəmanəmaŋa tua qinaʎan tua kaɉunaŋan.
S.FORM: saka zua vavayan a kakedrian puljatan tua nanemanemanga tua qinaljan tua kadjunangan.
S.PHON: saka zua vavajan a kakəɖian puʎatan tua nanəmanəmaŋa tua qinaʎan tua kaɉunaŋan.
S.TRANSL: The young girl had no inheritance of village or land left.

S.FORM: mangtjez azua tju Makazayazaya ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan kati Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu a kisudju tua zua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: maŋcəz azua cu makazajazaja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan kati kuʎəʎuʎəʎu a kisuɉu tua zua ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: mangtjez azua tju Makazayazaya ti Puljaljuljaljuyan kati Kuljeljuljelju a kisudju tua zua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz azua ʦu makazajazaja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan kati kuʎəʎuʎəʎu a kisuɉu tua zua ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan and Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu of Makazayazaya came to court Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: saka zazua ti sa Tjamuakakay patalaq azua.
S.PHON: saka zazua ti sa camuakakaj pataɭaq azua.
S.FORM: saka zazua ti sa Tjamuakakay patalaq azua.
S.PHON: saka zazua ti sa ʦamuakakaj pataɣaq azua.
S.TRANSL: So Tjamuakakay was jealous.

S.FORM: "aku nu kisudjuan zazua namaya?" ayain azua tia Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan kati Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.
S.PHON: "aku nu kisuɉuan zazua namaja" ajain azua tia puʎaʎuʎaʎujan kati kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.FORM: "aku nu kisudjuan zazua namaya?" ayain azua tia Puljaljuljaljuyan kati Kuljeljuljelju.
S.PHON: "aku nu kisuɉuan zazua namaja" ajain azua tia puʎaʎuʎaʎujan kati kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.TRANSL: “Why do you court someone like that?” she said to Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan and Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.

S.FORM: azua ti Tjukutjuku tjenglayen nua zua maḍusa.
S.PHON: azua ti cukucuku cəŋɭajən nua zua maɖusa.
S.FORM: azua ti Tjukutjuku tjenglayen nua zua madrusa.
S.PHON: azua ti ʦukuʦuku ʦəŋɣajən nua zua maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: But these two liked Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: vaik azua sema vavua mazelizeliuɫ.
S.PHON: vaik azua səma vavua mazəɭizəɭiuʎ.
S.FORM: vaik azua sema vavua mazelizeliulj.
S.PHON: vaik azua səma vavua mazəɣizəɣiuʎ.
S.TRANSL: They all went to the fields and worked together.

S.FORM: ka nadjemaɫun i vavua, "aku nu kizeliuɫan a namaya? savaqar tua kaḍaḍi tua nakuyan.
S.PHON: ka naɉəmaʎun i vavua, "aku nu kizəɭiuʎan a namajaʔ savaqar tua kaɖaɖi tua nakujan.
S.FORM: ka nadjemaljun i vavua, "aku nu kizeliuljan a namaya' savaqar tua kadradri tua nakuyan.
S.PHON: ka naɉəmaʎun i vavua, "aku nu kizəɣiuʎan a namajaʔ savaqaR tua kaɖaɖi tua nakujan.
S.TRANSL: When she got to the fields, (she said): “Why do you help someone like that with her work? She’s disgusting in her rags and ugliness.

S.FORM: sa-u tsikelu," ayain ni sa Tjamuakakay a maḍusa.
S.PHON: sa-u ʦikəɭu," ajain ni sa camuakakaj a maɖusa.
S.FORM: sa-u cikelu," ayain ni sa Tjamuakakay a madrusa.
S.PHON: sa-u ʦikəɣu," ajain ni sa ʦamuakakaj a maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: Go off home,” Tjamuakakay said to the two men.

S.FORM: manu ini kilangda azazua a matjelu.
S.PHON: manu ini kiɭaŋda azazua a macəɭu.
S.FORM: manu ini kilangda azazua a matjelu.
S.PHON: manu ini kiɣaŋda azazua a maʦəɣu.
S.TRANSL: But the three of them didn’t listen to her.

S.FORM: seman quma tua vasa, saka sema tapaw zazua kentsengeɫ.
S.PHON: səman quma tua vasa, saka səma tapaw zazua kənʦəŋəʎ.
S.FORM: seman quma tua vasa, saka sema tapaw zazua kencengelj.
S.PHON: səman quma tua vasa, saka səma tapaw zazua kənʦəŋəʎ.
S.TRANSL: They made a field of taro, and then went to the hut for lunch.

S.FORM: ka keman azua, "maya kidadut tua maḍusa; sasequ sun savaqar," ayain ni sa Tjamuakakay ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: ka kəman azua, "maja kidadut tua maɖusa; sasəqu sun savaqar," ajain ni sa camuakakaj ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: ka keman azua, "maya kidadut tua madrusa; sasequ sun savaqar," ayain ni sa Tjamuakakay ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: ka kəman azua, "maja kidadut tua maɖusa; sasəqu sun savaqaR," ajain ni sa ʦamuakakaj ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: When they were eating, Tjamuakakay said to Tjukutjuku: “Don’t go near those two. You smell and are disgusting.”

S.FORM: sa patjadaan ni sa Tjamuakakay azua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: sa pacadaan ni sa camuakakaj azua ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: sa patjadaan ni sa Tjamuakakay azua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: sa paʦadaan ni sa ʦamuakakaj azua ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: Tjamuakakay shared out food for Tjukutjuku;

S.FORM: saka nu patjada, sinu kakeḍian ni Tjukutjuku; avan a ni sa Tjamuakakay qatsaqatsan.
S.PHON: saka nu pacada, sinu kakəɖian ni cukucuku; avan a ni sa camuakakaj qaʦaqaʦan.
S.FORM: saka nu patjada, sinu kakedrian ni Tjukutjuku; avan a ni sa Tjamuakakay qacaqacan.
S.PHON: saka nu paʦada, sinu kakəɖian ni ʦukuʦuku; avan a ni sa ʦamuakakaj qaʦaqaʦan.
S.TRANSL: and when she did so the bits for Tjukutjuku were all small, and it was Tjamuakakay’s that were the big ones.

S.FORM: manu saka keman azua, sema zua i tjai Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu saka kəman azua, səma zua i cai cukucuku.
S.FORM: manu saka keman azua, sema zua i tjai Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu saka kəman azua, səma zua i ʦai ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: But when the two men ate they came to Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: sa ka makakan, vaik a sema kavavuan.
S.PHON: sa ka makakan, vaik a səma kavavuan.
S.FORM: sa ka makakan, vaik a sema kavavuan.
S.PHON: sa ka makakan, vaik a səma kavavuan.
S.TRANSL: When they had finished eating, they went back to the fields.

S.FORM: saka seleman anga a sema tapaw.
S.PHON: saka səɭəman aŋa a səma tapaw.
S.FORM: saka seleman anga a sema tapaw.
S.PHON: saka səɣəman aŋa a səma tapaw.
S.TRANSL: When it was getting dark they came to the hut.

S.FORM: ka uri vaik azua, "kasiuɫayu," ayain ti Tjukutjuku ni sa Tjamuakakay.
S.PHON: ka uri vaik azua, "kasiuʎaju," ajain ti cukucuku ni sa camuakakaj.
S.FORM: ka uri vaik azua, "kasiuljayu," ayain ti Tjukutjuku ni sa Tjamuakakay.
S.PHON: ka uRi vaik azua, "kasiuʎaju," ajain ti ʦukuʦuku ni sa ʦamuakakaj.
S.TRANSL: When they were going to go, Tjamuakakay said to Tjukutjuku; “Stay here!”

S.FORM: inika ui ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: inika ui ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: inika ui ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: inika ui ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku wouldn’t agree.

S.FORM: i vavaw tua tapaw a kalutskuts.
S.PHON: i vavaw tua tapaw a kaɭuʦkuʦ.
S.FORM: i vavaw tua tapaw a kaluckuc.
S.PHON: i vavaw tua tapaw a kaɣuʦkuʦ.
S.TRANSL: Tjamuakakay got on the hut and shouted.

S.FORM: "ari," ayain azua maḍusa.
S.PHON: "ari," ajain azua maɖusa.
S.FORM: "ari," ayain azua madrusa.
S.PHON: "aRi," ajain azua maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: “Let’s go!” she said to the two men.

S.FORM: manu qemaung sakamaya.
S.PHON: manu qəmauŋ sakamaja.
S.FORM: manu qemaung sakamaya.
S.PHON: manu qəmauŋ sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: And Tjukutjuku cried.

S.FORM: manu, "ari sakamaya," aya zazua maḍusa, ayain ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu, "ari sakamaja," aja zazua maɖusa, ajain ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: manu, "ari sakamaya," aya zazua madrusa, ayain ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu, "aRi sakamaja," aja zazua maɖusa, ajain ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: So those two said to Tjukutjuku: “Come on, let’s go.”

S.FORM: inika ui angata.
S.PHON: inika ui aŋata.
S.FORM: inika ui angata.
S.PHON: inika ui aŋata.
S.TRANSL: But she refused.

S.FORM: vaikan a sema umaq masalasaladj.
S.PHON: vaikan a səma umaq masaɭasaɭaɉ.
S.FORM: vaikan a sema umaq masalasaladj.
S.PHON: vaikan a səma umaq masaɣasaɣaɉ.
S.TRANSL: So they left her and went off home together.

S.FORM: manu kemɫang anga azua vuvu a sitsuayan a namatsay anga.
S.PHON: manu kəmʎaŋ aŋa azua vuvu a siʦuajan a namaʦaj aŋa.
S.FORM: manu kemljang anga azua vuvu a sicuayan a namacay anga.
S.PHON: manu kəmʎaŋ aŋa azua vuvu a siʦuajan a namaʦaj aŋa.
S.TRANSL: Now Tjukutjuku’s grandparent (grandfather, OA), who was long since dead, knew about it all.

S.FORM: mangtjez azua ti sa vuluvulung a venatsaq tua lukuɫ.
S.PHON: maŋcəz azua ti sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ a vənaʦaq tua ɭukuʎ.
S.FORM: mangtjez azua ti sa vuluvulung a venacaq tua lukulj.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz azua ti sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ a vənaʦaq tua ɣukuʎ.
S.TRANSL: And this old person came, carrying a box on a pole.

S.FORM: izua anemanemanga sipavay tjai Tjukutjuku, tua zangaq tua kaɫat tua situngan.
S.PHON: izua anəmanəmaŋa sipavaj cai cukucuku, tua zaŋaq tua kaʎat tua situŋan.
S.FORM: izua anemanemanga sipavay tjai Tjukutjuku, tua zangaq tua kaljat tua situngan.
S.PHON: izua anəmanəmaŋa sipavaj ʦai ʦukuʦuku, tua zaŋaq tua kaʎat tua situŋan.
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku was given all kinds of things-necklaces, bracelets and clothes.

S.FORM: "veritsan anga a su kava; nasa sakuya," ayain ni sa vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "vəriʦan aŋa a su kava; nasa sakuja," ajain ni sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: "verican anga a su kava; nasa sakuya," ayain ni sa vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "vəRiʦan aŋa a su kava; nasa sakuja," ajain ni sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Throw away your clothes; they seem nasty,” said the old person.

S.FORM: sa kipavalit anga azua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: sa kipavaɭit aŋa azua ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: sa kipavalit anga azua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: sa kipavaɣit aŋa azua ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: So Tjukutjuku changed her clothes.

S.FORM: saka "uri vaik aken," aya azua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: saka "uri vaik akən," aja azua vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: saka "uri vaik aken," aya azua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: saka "uRi vaik akən," aja azua vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: “I’m going,” said the old person.

S.FORM: "kitsiur aken," aya zua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: "kiʦiur akən," aja zua ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: "kiciur aken," aya zua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: "kiʦiuR akən," aja zua ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: “I’ll come with you,” said Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: manu "maya.
S.PHON: manu "maja.
S.FORM: manu "maya.
S.PHON: manu "maja.
S.TRANSL: “Don’t!

S.FORM: aku kitsiur sun? ɫa namatsay anga ken a sitsuayan," aya, saka inika ui.
S.PHON: aku kiʦiur sunʔ ʎa namaʦaj aŋa kən a siʦuajan," aja, saka inika ui.
S.FORM: aku kiciur sun' lja namacay anga ken a sicuayan," aya, saka inika ui.
S.PHON: aku kiʦiuR sunʔ ʎa namaʦaj aŋa kən a siʦuajan," aja, saka inika ui.
S.TRANSL: Why would you go with me? I’ve been dead a long time,” said the old person, who wouldn’t agree,

S.FORM: sa zua vaik anga sakamaya azua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: sa zua vaik aŋa sakamaja azua vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: sa zua vaik anga sakamaya azua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: sa zua vaik aŋa sakamaja azua vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: and just went off.

S.FORM: manu maɫianga zazua mangtjez anga kasi umaq azazua matjelu.
S.PHON: manu maʎiaŋa zazua maŋcəz aŋa kasi umaq azazua macəɭu.
S.FORM: manu maljianga zazua mangtjez anga kasi umaq azazua matjelu.
S.PHON: manu maʎiaŋa zazua maŋʦəz aŋa kasi umaq azazua maʦəɣu.
S.TRANSL: Next day the other three came from home.

S.FORM: saka nakemavanga nazemangaq anga nakemaɫat anga.
S.PHON: saka nakəmavaŋa nazəmaŋaq aŋa nakəmaʎat aŋa.
S.FORM: saka nakemavanga nazemangaq anga nakemaljat anga.
S.PHON: saka nakəmavaŋa nazəmaŋaq aŋa nakəmaʎat aŋa.
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku was wearing the new clothes, necklaces and bracelets.

S.FORM: saka "uki kasinu sa tsinakaw ni tsaqi," aya azua ti sa Tjamuakakay.
S.PHON: saka "uki kasinu sa ʦinakaw ni ʦaqi," aja azua ti sa camuakakaj.
S.FORM: saka "uki kasinu sa cinakaw ni caqi," aya azua ti sa Tjamuakakay.
S.PHON: saka "uki kasinu sa ʦinakaw ni ʦaqi," aja azua ti sa ʦamuakakaj.
S.TRANSL: “So where has the stinker stolen those?” said Tjamuakakay.

S.FORM: "ni vuvu a sinipavay taqezemetj.
S.PHON: "ni vuvu a sinipavaj taqəzəməc.
S.FORM: "ni vuvu a sinipavay taqezemetj.
S.PHON: "ni vuvu a sinipavaj taqəzəməʦ.
S.TRANSL: “The grandparent gave them to me last night,

S.FORM: 'kemɫang aken tu kinagalemgeman sun nia tjaina.
S.PHON: 'kəmʎaŋ akən tu kinagaɭəmgəman sun nia caina.
S.FORM: 'kemljang aken tu kinagalemgeman sun nia tjaina.
S.PHON: ʔkəmʎaŋ akən tu kinagaɣəmgəman sun nia ʦaina.
S.TRANSL: and said: ‘I know your aunt dislikes you.

S.FORM: (nu) tsemikel anga sun a sema umaq, qaqivu anga tua vali tua semuap tu su umaq,' ayain ni vuvu," aya ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: (nu) ʦəmikəɭ aŋa sun a səma umaq, qaqivu aŋa tua vaɭi tua səmuap tu su umaq,' ajain ni vuvu," aja ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: (nu) cemikel anga sun a sema umaq, qaqivu anga tua vali tua semuap tu su umaq,' ayain ni vuvu," aya ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: (nu) ʦəmikəɣ aŋa sun a səma umaq, qaqivu aŋa tua vaɣi tua səmuap tu su umaq,ʔ ajain ni vuvu," aja ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: When you go home, tell the wind to sweep your house,’ said the grandparent,” said Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: manu uri vaik anga azua.
S.PHON: manu uri vaik aŋa azua.
S.FORM: manu uri vaik anga azua.
S.PHON: manu uRi vaik aŋa azua.
S.TRANSL: When they were going to leave,

S.FORM: "vatsaqu azua ku nanemanemanga," aya ti Tjukutjuku tua zua maḍusa.
S.PHON: "vaʦaqu azua ku nanəmanəmaŋa," aja ti cukucuku tua zua maɖusa.
S.FORM: "vacaqu azua ku nanemanemanga," aya ti Tjukutjuku tua zua madrusa.
S.PHON: "vaʦaqu azua ku nanəmanəmaŋa," aja ti ʦukuʦuku tua zua maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku said to the two men: “Carry my things.”

S.FORM: manu "maya," ayain ni Tjamuakakay azua maḍusa.
S.PHON: manu "maja," ajain ni camuakakaj azua maɖusa.
S.FORM: manu "maya," ayain ni Tjamuakakay azua madrusa.
S.PHON: manu "maja," ajain ni ʦamuakakaj azua maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: “Don’t!” said Tjamuakakay to the two.

S.FORM: manu "vatsaqu sakamaya.
S.PHON: manu "vaʦaqu sakamaja.
S.FORM: manu "vacaqu sakamaya.
S.PHON: manu "vaʦaqu sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: “Just carry them.

S.FORM: inika ku tsinakaw; sinipavay ni vuvu," aya azua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: inika ku ʦinakaw; sinipavaj ni vuvu," aja azua ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: inika ku cinakaw; sinipavay ni vuvu," aya azua ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: inika ku ʦinakaw; sinipavaj ni vuvu," aja azua ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: I didn’t steal them. The grandparent gave them to me,” said Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: sa vatsaqi anga nazua maḍusa a djemaɫun i umaq.
S.PHON: sa vaʦaqi aŋa nazua maɖusa a ɉəmaʎun i umaq.
S.FORM: sa vacaqi anga nazua madrusa a djemaljun i umaq.
S.PHON: sa vaʦaqi aŋa nazua maɖusa a ɉəmaʎun i umaq.
S.TRANSL: So the two carried them back to the house.

S.FORM: pasa qaqivuin a vali, sa suapi a umaq ni Tjukutjuku, sa pasa qumaqani anga azua nanemanemanga.
S.PHON: pasa qaqivuin a vaɭi, sa suapi a umaq ni cukucuku, sa pasa qumaqani aŋa azua nanəmanəmaŋa.
S.FORM: pasa qaqivuin a vali, sa suapi a umaq ni Tjukutjuku, sa pasa qumaqani anga azua nanemanemanga.
S.PHON: pasa qaqivuin a vaɣi, sa suapi a umaq ni ʦukuʦuku, sa pasa qumaqani aŋa azua nanəmanəmaŋa.
S.TRANSL: She tried giving orders to the wind, and it swept Tjukutjuku’s house, and she put all her things into the house.

S.FORM: sa pasa puzayazayai a zaɫum a ḍiḍi.
S.PHON: sa pasa puzajazajai a zaʎum a ɖiɖi.
S.FORM: sa pasa puzayazayai a zaljum a dridri.
S.PHON: sa pasa puzajazajai a zaʎum a ɖiɖi.
S.TRANSL: And she put water and the pigs into the inner part.

S.FORM: sa kavaruk a "makuɫi a zaɫum," aya zazua.
S.PHON: sa kavaruk a "makuʎi a zaʎum," aja zazua.
S.FORM: sa kavaruk a "makulji a zaljum," aya zazua.
S.PHON: sa kavaRuk a "makuʎi a zaʎum," aja zazua.
S.TRANSL: And she swore: “The water has run dry.”

S.FORM: makuɫi a zaɫum, saka nekanga nu sikesa, nu tekelen.
S.PHON: makuʎi a zaʎum, saka nəkaŋa nu sikəsa, nu təkəɭən.
S.FORM: makulji a zaljum, saka nekanga nu sikesa, nu tekelen.
S.PHON: makuʎi a zaʎum, saka nəkaŋa nu sikəsa, nu təkəɣən.
S.TRANSL: The water ran dry, and there was none to cook with or to drink.

S.FORM: manu matsay anga a kakeḍian a ramaɫemaɫeng.
S.PHON: manu maʦaj aŋa a kakəɖian a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.FORM: manu macay anga a kakedrian a ramaljemaljeng.
S.PHON: manu maʦaj aŋa a kakəɖian a Ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.TRANSL: So children and old people died.

S.FORM: manu kiqaung anga sikataqaɫan.
S.PHON: manu kiqauŋ aŋa sikataqaʎan.
S.FORM: manu kiqaung anga sikataqaljan.
S.PHON: manu kiqauŋ aŋa sikataqaʎan.
S.TRANSL: When the villagers asked,

S.FORM: mangtjez azua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: maŋcəz azua vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: mangtjez azua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz azua vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: the old person came,

S.FORM: "pavayi aken tua zaɫum.
S.PHON: "pavaji akən tua zaʎum.
S.FORM: "pavayi aken tua zaljum.
S.PHON: "pavaji akən tua zaʎum.
S.TRANSL: and said: “Give me some water.

S.FORM: idan a su tjakumuɫ."
S.PHON: idan a su cakumuʎ."
S.FORM: idan a su tjakumulj."
S.PHON: idan a su ʦakumuʎ."
S.TRANSL: Give me your blanket.

S.FORM: sa tjevengi ni Tjukutjuku, sa pavayan anga tazua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: sa cəvəŋi ni cukucuku, sa pavajan aŋa tazua vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: sa tjevengi ni Tjukutjuku, sa pavayan anga tazua vuluvulung.
S.PHON: sa ʦəvəŋi ni ʦukuʦuku, sa pavajan aŋa tazua vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: And Tjukutjuku wetted it, and gave it to the old person.

S.FORM: manu maqepu a qaliqali a teptepan.
S.PHON: manu maqəpu a qaɭiqaɭi a təptəpan.
S.FORM: manu maqepu a qaliqali a teptepan.
S.PHON: manu maqəpu a qaɣiqaɣi a təptəpan.
S.TRANSL: Then others gathered round and sucked it.

S.FORM: saka megarang ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: saka məgaraŋ ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: saka megarang ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: saka məgaRaŋ ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku got annoyed.

S.FORM: "tsuqitsuqi," aya tua sikataqaɫan.
S.PHON: "ʦuqiʦuqi," aja tua sikataqaʎan.
S.FORM: "cuqicuqi," aya tua sikataqaljan.
S.PHON: "ʦuqiʦuqi," aja tua sikataqaʎan.
S.TRANSL: “That’s enough,” she said to the villagers.

S.FORM: manu "nia patsevudi tua zaɫum a su qinaɫan," aya.
S.PHON: manu "nia paʦəvudi tua zaʎum a su qinaʎan," aja.
S.FORM: manu "nia pacevudi tua zaljum a su qinaljan," aya.
S.PHON: manu "nia paʦəvudi tua zaʎum a su qinaʎan," aja.
S.TRANSL: “We’ll give your village a source of water.”

S.FORM: tsevud anga a zaɫum; sa mirazek anga a qinaɫan, aya a
S.PHON: ʦəvud aŋa a zaʎum; sa mirazək aŋa a qinaʎan, aja a
S.FORM: cevud anga a zaljum; sa mirazek anga a qinaljan, aya a
S.PHON: ʦəvud aŋa a zaʎum; sa miRazək aŋa a qinaʎan, aja a
S.TRANSL: tjautsikel.
